reason: Response B (circle) shows greater rhetorical sophistication and richer, more varied evidence — distinguishing axes of symmetry across square/hexagon/circle, marshaling diverse natural examples, and structuring a layered "emotional, mathematical, physical, perceptual" argument that it then explicitly synthesizes. Response A (sphere) is competent and clean but more formulaic and repetitive (restating "restful/completeness" several ways), which reads more like Haiku's tidier but thinner output. The denser conceptual control and self-aware framing in B better fit the mid-tier Sonnet.
